title: Executive Summary
content:
BA closes 2025-12-31 with $89.5B of revenue (34.5% YoY) and $2.23B of net income versus -$11.8B in the prior period. The period ended with an operating margin of 4.8% and a net margin of 2.5%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability.

Operating cash flow reached $1.06B versus -$12.1B in the prior period, and after $2.94B of capital spending the business produced -$1.88B of free cash flow (-86.9% YoY). Capital spending absorbed 3.3% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Current liquidity of 1.19x and debt-to-equity of 11.42x point to a balance sheet that is neither clearly stressed nor clearly overcapitalized. That balance-sheet reading should still be weighed against weak cash generation when operating or free cash flow is negative.

The dashboard uses the same row order across reports, and values the filing does not disclose remain explicitly marked rather than being hidden.
*Asset Turnover: Asset turnover uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Book Value Per Share: Book Value Per Share uses period-end shares outstanding.*
*Earnings Per Share (Diluted): Earnings Per Share (Diluted) uses diluted weighted-average shares from the income statement.*
*Return on Assets: ROA uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Return on Equity: ROE uses average equity for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*

title: Trend Analysis
content:
Across the annual periods shown here, operating cash flow provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $1.06B and a compound annual growth rate of -11.5%. The decline is gradual, but the direction remains negative across the observed periods.

The multi-year trajectory has been broadly stable without a strong directional shift. Cash generation remains the main point of pressure, with $1.06B of operating cash flow and -$1.88B of free cash flow in the current period. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (11.42x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (1.19x current ratio), because those measures do not always point to the same conclusion about financial flexibility.

title: Risk Factors
content: The most important risks in the current numbers are concentrated in the areas where profitability, cash generation, or balance-sheet support are not moving together. Debt-to-equity at 11.42x indicates elevated leverage.

title: Analyst Notes
content:
The most constructive part of BA's profile is straightforward: ROE of 289.7% points to very strong earnings generation relative to book equity. Revenue rose 34.5% year over year, marking a meaningful step-up in scale. That matters because leverage (11.42x debt-to-equity), liquidity (1.19x current ratio), and free cash flow (-$1.88B) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-12-31, with the prior period set at 2024-12-31. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.
